title: Large Marge

jokes.text: Dad, I'm telling you, Truman wins. / Woo-hoo! I win the bet. Who's your daddy?
jokes.setup: Lisa confidently predicts Truman will win the 1948 election
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrates winning a bet before the actual results are revealed
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with historical irony, Homer's premature celebration is character-appropriate

jokes.text: We're building homes for the homeless. [GASPS] You know it's gonna be bad, but you just can't prepare yourself.
jokes.setup: Lisa announces they'll be doing volunteer work
jokes.punchline: Homer's horrified reaction to doing charity work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - treating charity work like impending doom

jokes.text: Domiciles for the Destitute? Is this that thing Jimmy Can'ter does?
jokes.setup: Reading the charity organization name
jokes.punchline: Homer mispronounces 'Carter' as 'Can'ter'
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer mispronunciation humor, fits character but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'll slaughter you, you lamebrain. / No hard feelings.
jokes.setup: Bush Sr. threatens violence against Carter
jokes.punchline: Carter responds with cheerful politeness to death threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between violent threat and Carter's famously nice demeanor

jokes.text: This is a Band-Aid wrapped in tinfoil. / My real ring's inside a turtle.
jokes.setup: Lisa notices Homer's 'wedding ring' is fake
jokes.punchline: Homer's increasingly absurd explanation about where his real ring is
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation - starts with simple deception, ends with turtle

jokes.text: And I don't wanna spend another fiscal year alone. Un!
jokes.setup: Lindsay Naegle introducing herself romantically
jokes.punchline: Using business terminology ('fiscal year') for romantic loneliness
jokes.explanation: Perfect character integration - corporate executive can't separate business and personal life

jokes.text: Cookie Kwan, realtor. Number one on the west side. I could move you into a beautiful new home. Mine.
jokes.setup: Cookie introduces herself as a realtor offering services
jokes.punchline: The 'home' she's selling is her own for romantic purposes
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection using real estate pitch for pickup line

jokes.text: Cookie Kwan, realtor. Number one on the west side. I could move you into a beautiful new home. Mine.
jokes.setup: Cookie introduces herself as a realtor
jokes.punchline: Uses real estate metaphor to proposition Homer
jokes.explanation: Decent character-specific pickup line using her profession

jokes.text: Who left the milk out? Yuck, yuck, yuck, yuck Dad left the milk out Yuck, yuck, yuck, yuck
jokes.setup: Radio Disney advertises songs rewritten for babies
jokes.punchline: Baha Men's 'Who Let the Dogs Out' becomes about spoiled milk
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture parody with domestic twist

jokes.text: That's good satire. It doesn't hurt anyone.
jokes.setup: Radio Disney parody song playing
jokes.punchline: Marge's naive assessment of toothless corporate parody as 'good satire'
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on corporate-safe parody through Marge's innocent perspective

jokes.text: Well, I've read enough articles about flirting to know it when I see it.
jokes.setup: Marge observing Homer grunting and working
jokes.punchline: Marge misinterprets construction work as women flirting with Homer
jokes.explanation: Marge's insecurity making her see threats where none exist, good character work

jokes.text: Hey, why would I want Purina when I got Fancy Feast right here?
jokes.setup: Marge asking if Homer thinks about other women
jokes.punchline: Homer comparing women to pet food brands
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at romance backfires through inappropriate metaphor - very in-character

jokes.text: Well, if it isn't Bat-mensch and the Goy Wonder.
jokes.setup: Krusty as villain confronting Batman and Robin
jokes.punchline: Jewish-themed puns on superhero names
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay combining Batman references with Krusty's Jewish identity - highly quotable

jokes.text: Well, sure. He was also Uncle Velderschmoink on Bewitched.
jokes.setup: Lisa surprised Krusty played Batman villain
jokes.punchline: Bart mentions another fake TV credit with ridiculous name
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with fake TV credit

jokes.text: What in the name of Huntley and Brinkley do you plan to do to us?
jokes.setup: Batman asking villain about his plan
jokes.punchline: Using 1960s news anchors as a Batman-style oath
jokes.explanation: Good period-appropriate reference for 1960s Batman show parody

jokes.text: Fortunately, I always carry my Carousel Reversal Spray.
jokes.setup: Batman needs to escape from spinning death trap
jokes.punchline: Impossibly specific solution conveniently available in utility belt
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of Batman's convenient solutions - absurdly specific gadget

jokes.text: Faced with a problem like this, I wonder, what would Oprah do?
jokes.setup: Marge confronting her body image issues
jokes.punchline: Treating Oprah as a spiritual guide for weight loss
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference for early 2000s Oprah influence on middle-class women

jokes.text: My maguppies became bazongas.
jokes.setup: Marge discovers she got breast implants by mistake
jokes.punchline: Uses childish euphemisms for breasts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge language - innocent euphemisms for awkward situation

jokes.text: My maguppies became bazongas.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining the surgical mix-up
jokes.punchline: Using cutesy euphemisms for breast enlargement
jokes.explanation: Marge's innocent way of describing her situation with made-up words

jokes.text: Wait, this just doesn't add up. Milhouse has never been anything but a comic foil.
jokes.setup: Milhouse getting in trouble for flag incident
jokes.punchline: Bart analyzing Milhouse's role using TV writing terminology
jokes.explanation: Excellent meta-humor with Bart breaking fourth wall to analyze show's own character dynamics

jokes.text: The spritz has hit the fan.
jokes.setup: Situation escalating with flag incident
jokes.punchline: Bowdlerized version of 'shit hits the fan' using 'spritz'
jokes.explanation: Decent censored version of common expression, fits Skinner's character

jokes.text: Your endowment's bigger than Harvard's. / Well, that cinches it. Lisa gets the prize for the best off-the-cuff response.
jokes.setup: Lisa commenting on Marge's enhanced chest
jokes.punchline: Homer judging Lisa's wordplay like a comedy competition
jokes.explanation: Clever double entendre with 'endowment' plus Homer's meta-commentary on wordplay quality

jokes.text: Boy, oh, Boyardee. / Ha, ha. I bazoom you want a table. Right this way.
jokes.setup: Restaurant host seeing Marge's enhanced figure
jokes.punchline: Multiple puns incorporating brand names and 'bazoom' for breasts
jokes.explanation: Good rapid-fire wordplay with Italian stereotype character making breast puns

jokes.text: [SINGING] You took a 20-carat diamond And made it gleam
jokes.setup: Italian restaurant staff breaking into song about Marge
jokes.punchline: Elaborate musical number praising her enhanced figure
jokes.explanation: Full musical number is ambitious and absurd, good execution of unexpected format

jokes.text: The hottest thing to hit this city Since the fire that killed 11 [ALL GASPING] Dangerous criminals [IN UNISON] Hooray!
jokes.setup: Complimenting Marge as hottest thing to hit Springfield
jokes.punchline: Dark turn about deadly fire, then relief it only killed criminals
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy misdirection - starts with tragedy, pivots to moral relief

jokes.text: Forget it, Kiekie. You're not putting your brain into her body.
jokes.setup: Modeling agent wanting to work with Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's paranoid misunderstanding of modeling industry
jokes.explanation: Homer's absurd interpretation shows his lack of understanding but protective instincts

jokes.text: With the economy the way it is, you're lucky you get soup.
jokes.setup: Bart complaining he doesn't get a song like Marge did
jokes.punchline: Homer treating songs like limited economic resources
jokes.explanation: Homer applying economic logic to entertainment privileges in silly but character-consistent way

jokes.text: Yeah. This is the longest I've ever gone without looking at Lenny.
jokes.setup: Barney commenting that Homer is lucky
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre measure of how distracted he is by Marge
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd way to measure attention span - implies he normally stares at Lenny constantly

jokes.text: Teletubbies get away with more than this.
jokes.setup: Complaining about restrictions on Krusty's show
jokes.punchline: Comparing children's show standards to adult clown show
jokes.explanation: Good cultural reference contrasting wholesome kids' TV with comedy show censorship

jokes.text: Maybe if we cut his foot off, people will feel sorry for him. / It didn't help your dad get your mom back.
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting extreme sympathy-generating tactics
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's dark reference to his parents' divorce
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation with Milhouse's unexpectedly brutal personal reference

jokes.text: No, I got where I am by naming names in the '50s.
jokes.setup: Krusty saying he didn't get where he is by giving up
jokes.punchline: Revealing he informed on people during McCarthyism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark revelation about Krusty's past that's both historically relevant and character-revealing

jokes.text: My forthrightness is my undoing.
jokes.setup: Marge called the shoes ugly in endorsement
jokes.punchline: Pretentious way to describe her honesty problem
jokes.explanation: Good Marge character moment - overly formal language for simple concept

jokes.text: Whoa, he's a big as Brando, but he takes direction.
jokes.setup: Seeing Stampy the elephant perform
jokes.punchline: Compares elephant to notoriously difficult actor Marlon Brando
jokes.explanation: Clever pop culture reference about difficult actors

jokes.text: Columbo! No, that's not it. Modesto! Daktari? Nintendo? Oh.
jokes.setup: Krusty trying to remember elephant's safety word
jokes.punchline: Increasingly random guesses including TV shows and video games
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of panic with increasingly absurd guesses that show Krusty's scattered mind

jokes.text: Sorry, ma'am. We don't negotiate with elephants.
jokes.setup: Marge asking police not to shoot elephant with people inside
jokes.punchline: Treating elephant hostage situation with bureaucratic indifference
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan line treating absurd situation with bureaucratic logic

jokes.text: Hey, cops. Check out this all-points bulletin.
jokes.setup: Marge needs to distract police from shooting
jokes.punchline: Using her enhanced chest as 'bulletin' pun
jokes.explanation: Clever pun combining police terminology with visual distraction

jokes.text: Whoa, look at those magumbos. [TRUMPETS] Hey, that's it. Magumbo!
jokes.setup: Cop creates new slang for Marge's breasts
jokes.punchline: Accidentally says the elephant's safety word
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that resolves crisis through accidental wordplay

jokes.text: I came out of the elephant's mouth, right? Because I already showered once today.
jokes.setup: Homer emerging from elephant after rescue
jokes.punchline: More concerned about hygiene than near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - personal comfort over dramatic circumstances

tmdb_overview: Thinking Homer does not find her attractive anymore, Marge decides to get liposuction, but instead receives breast implants. She becomes adored by many of the men in Springfield, and becomes a spokesmodel for trade shows. Meanwhile, Bart and Milhouse try to imitate a stunt they saw on an episode of Batman that guest stars Krusty the Clown. When the stunt ends badly, media watchdog groups blame Krusty, forcing the clown to make his show more safety-conscious and less fun.
